findings.title: Residuals carve-out is too broad
findings.clause: each Party may use for any purpose the ideas, concepts, know-how, and techniques retained in the unaided memory of its personnel
findings.rationale: The unaided-memory residuals right permits product development from confidential information and hollows out the NDA. Narrow to general skills and experience, or reject.

findings.title: One-year term with no trade-secret carve-out
findings.clause: shall remain in effect for one (1) year from the Effective Date
findings.rationale: A flat one year duration falls below the two-year floor and leaves trade secrets unprotected after year one. Extend to 3-5 years with a perpetual trade secret carve-out.

findings.title: Marking-only definition creates an oral-disclosure gap
findings.clause: Information disclosed orally or visually is protected only if reduced to a writing marked Confidential within five business days.
findings.rationale: A marking requirement leaves oral and visual disclosures in demos and meetings unprotected unless re-papered in a narrow window.

findings.title: Return and destruction obligation is weak
findings.clause: Upon written request, the receiving Party will use commercially reasonable efforts to return or destroy Confidential Information.
findings.rationale: Return/destruction should trigger on termination, be mandatory within 30 days, and require officer certification rather than commercially reasonable efforts on request.

findings.title: Compelled disclosure has no notice requirement
findings.clause: If required by law to disclose Confidential Information, it may do so without liability under this Agreement.
findings.rationale: Compelled disclosure with no prompt notice denies the disclosing Party any opportunity to seek a protective order.
